segments.text: There is a field where freshers are earning up to 10 lakhs, but 70% of applicants are still not getting hired.
Most people think that AI or ML engineering means being able to prompt Chad GPT. Well, but that's also like thinking that if you know how to use
Google, it qualifies you to build Google. So, here is what an AI ML engineer actually does. They build the systems that learn. So, they decide how
raw data can become intelligent models to solve real world problems. For example, Netflix's recommendation feature, fraud detection in banking
apps, or your voice recognition in Alexa. All of those don't exist without ML engineers designing how the system learns from millions of data points. So,
chat GPD is just the base product. ML engineers design how the product will learn and you too can learn this skill
completely free of cost. First is begin with your Python basics, but do not skip your linear algebra and statistics.
Search for Andrew Neng machine learning free code camp on YouTube. Google's ML crash course is also completely free.
Then move to building real models.
Practice classification and regression on actual data sets using scikitlearn.
Anyone can train a model on a clean data set. But the real skill is ML ops. Only after that do you touch deep learning,
TensorFlow or PyTorch, neural networks and training models at scale. In this role, freshers start around 5 to 10
lakhs perom. But with experience that can jump to 18 to 35 lakhs per anomo. The world does not need more prompters.
It needs more builders. Follow full disclosure for more such gone debt.
